methods.description: This method involves using the Ice Sheet System Model (ISSM) to simulate ice flow dynamics using the Shallow Ice Approximation (SIA) for the Hardangerjøkulen ice cap. The SIA is chosen for its computational efficiency and suitability for modeling ice cap evolution over long time scales.

steps.description: We use the Ice Sheet System Model (ISSM), a finite element ice flow model primarily developed for high-resolution, higher-order modeling of ice sheets using a parallel software architecture.
steps.searchable_summary: utilize ISSM for high-resolution ice flow modeling

steps.description: We use the two-dimensional (2d), vertically integrated Shallow Ice Approximation (SIA) within ISSM, meaning horizontal velocities correspond to averaged velocities over the ice column.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 2D SIA within ISSM for ice flow simulation

steps.description: The SIA is based on a scaling analysis of the full Stokes stress balance, assuming typical glacier length L is much larger than the typical ice thickness H.
steps.searchable_summary: implement SIA based on scaling analysis of Stokes stress balance

steps.description: For Hardangerjøkulen, the characteristic horizontal scale is 4 to 8 km, and the characteristic ice thickness ∼200 m, giving an aspect-ratio of 0.05 to 0.025, which is acceptable for using the SIA.
steps.searchable_summary: validate SIA applicability for Hardangerjøkulen based on aspect-ratio

steps.description: The SIA is computationally efficient, enabling modeling of ice cap evolution over long time scales and for a wide parameter space.
steps.searchable_summary: model ice cap evolution using computationally efficient SIA
